meta.summary: Credit-based chat app: users buy credit packs, book a real future time slot from the expert's calendar (browsable via a dedicated Slot Finder page across all experts), and chat with a server-authoritative timer that bills overage per minute. Auth (incl. Google, forgot/reset password, and change-password/set-password from the profile page), credit balance, ledger history, a birth-details profile, real calendar-based booking with an optional note + email notification to the expert, cancel/reschedule, the full session billing loop, a basic expert role with an admin page (manage availability + all bookings), a per-booking past-chats history, and real-time persisted chat messaging (Pusher, with .txt export, persistent input focus, and a soft-Leave/hard-End-Session split so accidental disconnects are recoverable) are all live end-to-end against Postgres. Only the credit *purchase* loop (Razorpay) is still mocked/design-only.
meta.note: This file is hand-updated after each meaningful change. See PROJECT_TRACKER.md for narrative notes.

pages.title: Landing Page
pages.description: Marketing hero, mock chat preview, and features grid. Fully static.

pages.title: Pricing & Credit Packs
pages.description: Three credit packs (Starter/Pro/Expert) rendered from hardcoded JSX. 'Buy Now' buttons have no onClick handler yet.

pages.title: Log In
pages.description: Email/password form. Calls next-auth signIn('credentials', ...) and redirects to /app/dashboard on success; shows 'Invalid email or password.' on failure. Also has a 'Continue with Google' button (signIn('google', ...)) and a 'Forgot password?' link (/user/forgot-password, added 2026-07-04).

pages.title: Sign Up
pages.description: Name/email/password/confirm form. Client-side password-match check, then POSTs to /api/register to create the user, then auto signs in and redirects to /app/dashboard. Also has a 'Continue with Google' button.

pages.title: Forgot Password
pages.description: New. Client component, email-only form. POSTs to /api/forgot-password and always renders the same generic success copy once submitted, regardless of whether the email matched an account -- this is the actual non-enumeration security property, not incidental UX. No resubmission after success (a UX nicety only; the endpoint itself has no rate limiting -- see knownGaps).

pages.title: Reset Password
pages.description: New. Client component. Reads `token` from the URL via useSearchParams(), which requires wrapping the reading component in a <Suspense> boundary (a real Next.js 15 App Router build requirement, confirmed by building this page). New-password + confirm fields with the same client-side match check as the signup page. POSTs to /api/reset-password; on success redirects to /user/login. Shows 'Invalid reset link.' immediately, with no API call, if the token param is missing.

pages.title: Dashboard
pages.description: Server component: redirects to /user/login if unauthenticated, then branches on session.user.role. Clients see the greeting, real credit balance, and real upcoming bookings only (BookingActions island -- slot browsing/booking moved to the dedicated /app/slots page, decluttering this view now that booking has a note-taking step); Quick Actions now has four real links: 'Find a Slot' (/app/slots, replacing the old dead 'Find Expert' button), 'Ledger History' (/app/ledger), 'Profile' (/app/profile), and 'Past Chats' (/app/chats). Experts (role='expert') see a completely different view: a 'Client Sessions' panel (ExpertSessions island) for joining active sessions, plus links to /app/admin (calendar/bookings) and /app/chats (all past sessions across every client).

pages.title: Find a Slot
pages.description: New page. Server component, auth-guarded. Calls listOpenSlotsWithExpertNames() (every open future slot across all experts, expert name joined in) and getUserProfile() (to flag which birth-detail fields are missing), grouped by calendar date and rendered by the SlotFinder island. This is where booking actually happens now -- clicking a slot reveals an inline note textarea before confirming, instead of the old instant-book flow.

pages.title: Profile
pages.description: Server component, auth-guarded (any role). Fetches getUserProfile() and renders two panels: ProfileForm (name, date of birth, time of birth, place of birth -- never a gate, all optional, saved via PATCH /api/user/profile; used to give the expert astrological context for a booking, surfaced in the booking-notification email) and, as of 2026-07-04, ChangePasswordForm (POSTs to /api/user/password, fed `hasPassword` from getUserProfile() to decide whether to show a 'Change Password' or 'Set a Password' variant).

pages.title: Past Chats
pages.description: New page. Server component, auth-guarded, branches on role like the dashboard does: clients see their own past (ended) chat_sessions (listPastSessionsForClient); experts see EVERY past session across every client (listPastSessionsForExpert), mirroring the same 'own vs. all' asymmetry as listAssignedBookings/listAllAssignedBookings. Each row links to the existing (already read-only once ended) /app/chat/[id] page plus the existing .txt export endpoint -- no new lib logic needed for either. Pure server component, no client island.

pages.title: Active Chat Session
pages.description: Server component: redirects to /user/login if unauthenticated, calls tickSession() and listMessages() server-side (id is a real chat_sessions UUID) for authoritative initial billing state + message history, 404s via notFound() if the session doesn't exist or the caller is neither the paying client nor the assigned expert. Now also calls assertSessionAccess() to compute isViewerExpert, passed to ChatWindow so it can hide client-only credit/balance language from the expert's view. Session billing is fully real and always reflects the client's wallet regardless of which participant is viewing. Chat messages are persisted to Postgres and delivered live over a private Pusher channel to both participants.

pages.title: Ledger History
pages.description: Server component, auth-guarded. Lists the signed-in user's ledger_entries (purchase/slot_booking/overage_charge/refund) newest-first via listLedgerEntries(), with a human label per entry_type, the description, timestamp, and the signed amount (green for credits added, red for credits spent). Linked from the dashboard's 'Ledger History' quick action.

pages.title: Admin
pages.description: Server component: redirects to /user/login if unauthenticated, redirects to /app/dashboard if role !== 'expert' (reuses the expert role as admin rights -- no separate admin role exists). Two panels: Availability (AvailabilityManager island -- create a new calendar slot, view/cancel existing ones) and All Bookings (AdminBookingsList island -- every booking ever assigned to this expert regardless of status, with cancel-always-100%-refund and reschedule-with-no-lead-time-restriction actions). Linked from the expert's dashboard and from Header's conditional 'Admin' nav link.

components.name: Header
components.description: Global nav bar. Auth-aware (shows Login/Signup vs Dashboard/Logout) via next-auth's useSession, wrapped in its own SessionProvider.

components.name: ChatWindow
components.description: Billing polls POST /api/sessions/[id]/tick every 5s and renders the server's authoritative status (active/grace/ended), balance, and grace countdown. New isViewerExpert prop drives two things: the ended/grace status copy hides all credit/balance language (and the 'Top Up Credits' button) for an expert viewer, since that's about the client's wallet, not theirs. The single old 'Leave' button (which permanently ended the session) is now split into 'Leave' (soft -- pure client-side router.push, no API call, session/booking state untouched so either party can just Join again to resume) and 'End Session' (hard -- window.confirm-guarded, calls the same end endpoint as before, permanently marks the booking 'completed'). The message input now keeps focus after every send via a ref + a useEffect that re-focuses once the disabled state clears (previously required re-clicking the box each time). Messaging is real: starts from server-fetched initialMessages, subscribes to the session's private Pusher channel (getPusherClient()) and appends incoming 'new-message' events (deduped by id -- no optimistic local append). Own vs. other messages are distinguished by comparing senderId to the viewerId prop rather than a fixed role. 'Export Chat' is a plain link to the export endpoint.

components.name: BookingActions
components.description: Client island rendered on the client's dashboard. Now purely an upcoming-bookings management component (booking new slots moved to the SlotFinder island on /app/slots). Lists existing upcoming bookings (each showing the assigned expert's name) with: a Join button that's disabled until within 10 minutes of the booking's real scheduled starts_at (client-side display gate, recomputed every 30s; the server enforces the real check via TooEarlyToJoinError); a Cancel button for 'scheduled' bookings (POST /api/bookings/[id]/cancel, tiered refund shown inline); and a Reschedule action shown only when the booking is more than 3 days out (inline <select> of the expert's other open same-type slots, POST /api/bookings/[id]/reschedule). Still receives the dashboard's plain listOpenSlots() as a `slots` prop, but only to populate that reschedule picker.

components.name: SlotFinder
components.description: New client island on /app/slots. Renders every open slot across all experts, grouped by calendar date, each showing expert name/type/time/price. Clicking a slot's Book button reveals an inline note <textarea> ('your questions or the areas of life you'd like to discuss') + Confirm button, reusing BookingActions' inline-reveal interaction shape rather than an instant-book click. If the viewer's profile is missing DOB/time/place, shows a non-blocking hint suggesting they fill in their profile or just include the details in the note. POSTs {availabilitySlotId, note} to POST /api/bookings.

components.name: ProfileForm
components.description: Client island on /app/profile. Controlled form for name/date-of-birth/time-of-birth/place-of-birth, PATCHes /api/user/profile. No required-field validation -- saving is never a gate. Normalizes Postgres DATE/TIME values (which node-postgres returns as JS Date objects, not strings, for DATE columns) into plain YYYY-MM-DD / HH:MM strings for the <input type='date'/'time'> elements -- done in users.ts's rowToProfile() using local-date getters (not toISOString(), which shifts the calendar date backward by a day in any timezone ahead of UTC; caught and fixed during this feature's verification).

components.name: ChangePasswordForm
components.description: New client island on /app/profile, rendered as a second panel below ProfileForm. Takes a `hasPassword` prop: if true, shows 'Change Password' with a required Current Password field, verified server-side via bcrypt; if false (a Google-only account), shows 'Set a Password' with no current-password field at all and an explanatory note that this also enables email/password login. Both variants have New/Confirm fields with a client-side match check (rejects with zero network call on mismatch) before POSTing to /api/user/password. Clears all three fields and shows a success notice on completion rather than leaving stale values in a password field.

components.name: ExpertSessions
components.description: Client island rendered on the expert's dashboard (role='expert'). Lists bookings assigned to the expert (client name/email, slot length, time). Shows a 'Join Session' button only once the client has actually started the session (booking status 'active' -- a chat_sessions row exists); otherwise shows 'Waiting for client'. Joining calls the same POST /api/sessions/join used by clients, which for an expert caller only ever attaches to an existing session, never creates/bills one. Unaffected by the calendar/availability feature -- still shows only scheduled/active bookings, day-to-day join flow only.

components.name: AvailabilityManager
components.description: Client island on /app/admin. A create form (slot-type dropdown + datetime-local input, converted to an ISO string client-side to avoid timezone ambiguity) posts to POST /api/admin/availability-slots. Lists all of the expert's slots (open/booked/cancelled, dimmed when not open) with a Cancel action for open ones only (POST /api/admin/availability-slots/[id]/cancel) -- a booked slot can't be pulled directly here, only via cancelling/rescheduling the booking that claimed it.

components.name: AdminBookingsList
components.description: Client island on /app/admin. Every booking ever assigned to the expert, any status, with a status badge. For 'scheduled' bookings: Cancel (always shows a 100% refund result, since this path is always expert-initiated) and Reschedule (same same-type-slot picker pattern as BookingActions, but with no 3-day lead-time restriction -- the expert can reschedule however soon the session is).

components.name: db (pool + withTransaction)
components.description: Singleton node-postgres Pool reading DATABASE_URL from .env.local (gitignored), cached on globalThis in dev. Also exports withTransaction() (BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK helper) used by bookings.ts and sessions.ts for atomic multi-statement writes.

components.name: users lib
components.description: createUser() and verifyUser() against the real users table. Passwords hashed with bcryptjs (10 rounds); duplicate email throws EmailInUseError (mapped to Postgres unique-violation code 23505). verifyUser() returns null if password_hash is NULL (Google-only accounts can't log in via Credentials). findOrCreateOAuthUser(email, name): atomic upsert (INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (email) DO UPDATE) used by the Google provider. PublicUser now includes role ('user' | 'expert', default 'user'), propagated through NextAuth into session.user.role. Deliberately separate UserProfile type + getUserProfile()/updateUserProfile() (name/dateOfBirth/timeOfBirth/placeOfBirth) -- kept out of PublicUser/the JWT session by design. Also getUserContact() (email/name only) used by the booking email. New (2026-07-04): UserProfile.hasPassword (derived `password_hash IS NOT NULL`, drives the profile page's Change-vs-Set-Password UI) and changePassword(userId, {currentPassword?, newPassword}) -- verifies currentPassword via bcrypt.compare if the account already has a password_hash (throws InvalidCurrentPasswordError on mismatch/omission), or skips that check entirely if password_hash is NULL (a Google-only account setting its first password, a state verifyUser() already anticipated).

components.name: GoogleIcon
components.description: Shared multi-color 'G' SVG icon used by the 'Continue with Google' buttons on both login and signup pages.

components.name: ledger lib
components.description: getBalance(userId) -> SUM(amount) over ledger_entries, coalesced to 0. listLedgerEntries(userId) -> full history newest-first, powers /app/ledger. Read-only; ledger_entries is written by bookings.ts (slot_booking, refund) and sessions.ts (overage_charge) in addition to manual/test inserts.

components.name: authOptions
components.description: Shared NextAuthOptions (moved out of the route handler) so server components and API routes can call getServerSession(authOptions) to identify the current user.

components.name: sessionRules
components.description: Constants matching session_rules.md: SLOT_TYPES (standard: 50cr/30min, extended: 90cr/60min -- deliberately kept hardcoded, dynamic per-tier pricing was scoped out of the calendar/availability feature), OVERAGE_RATE_PER_MINUTE (2), LOW_BALANCE_WARNING_CREDITS (4), GRACE_PERIOD_MS (2min), CANCELLATION_FULL_REFUND_WINDOW_MS/CANCELLATION_PARTIAL_REFUND_PERCENT (client-initiated cancellation tiers only), JOIN_WINDOW_BEFORE_MS (10min -- how early a client can join a scheduled session), RESCHEDULE_MIN_LEAD_TIME_MS (3 days -- client-initiated reschedule lead time; the expert is exempt). Also the shared SessionState type used by both the server lib and the client ChatWindow.

components.name: availability lib
components.description: The expert's calendar. createAvailabilitySlot(expertId, {slotType, startsAt}): validates startsAt is in the future, rejects overlapping slots for that expert, inserts status='open'. listOpenSlots(): all open future slots across the (one) expert, feeds BookingActions' reschedule picker. New listOpenSlotsWithExpertNames(): same but joins in the expert's name, feeds the /app/slots Slot Finder page (this is the query that's actually multi-expert-ready, even though only one expert exists today). listAllSlotsForExpert(expertId): every status, for the admin calendar view. cancelAvailabilitySlot(expertId, slotId): only for a still-'open' slot. Shares SlotNotFoundError/SlotNotAvailableError/SlotOverlapError/ForbiddenError with bookings.ts (single source there, re-exported here) to avoid a circular import between the two modules.

components.name: bookings lib
components.description: createBooking(userId, availabilitySlotId, note?): per-user advisory lock + transaction, locks and validates the target availability_slots row (must be 'open' and in the future -- SlotNotAvailableError otherwise), checks balance against the (still-hardcoded) SLOT_TYPES cost for that slot's type, marks the slot 'booked', and inserts the booking (snapshotting starts_at/ends_at/included_minutes/slot_type/availability_slot_id, plus the new optional note text) + a slot_booking ledger debit atomically. The Resend email trigger deliberately lives in the API route, not here -- this module stays a pure DB-transaction boundary, same as before. listUpcomingBookings/listAssignedBookings/listAllAssignedBookings unchanged. cancelBooking(callerId, bookingId): authorizes the client OR the assigned expert; refund policy branches on *who* -- expert-initiated is always 100%, client-initiated keeps the tiered policy; refund always credits booking.user_id, never callerId; releases the tied availability_slots row back to 'open'. rescheduleBooking(callerId, bookingId, newAvailabilitySlotId): same dual authorization; client-initiated reschedules require >3 days' lead time, the expert is exempt; new slot must be open/future/same-type; no ledger entries (pure time change).

components.name: sessions lib
components.description: The core billing engine, dual-access. joinSession(callerId, bookingId): if the caller is the booking's client and no session exists yet, enforces a join-time gate first (TooEarlyToJoinError otherwise), then creates the chat_sessions row and bills them; resuming an already-active session skips the gate -- this is also what makes rejoining after an accidental soft-Leave work, with zero new code. If the caller is the assigned expert, only ever attaches to an *already-existing* session and never creates or bills one. tickSession(callerId, sessionId) authorizes either party but always resolves a separate 'payerId' (= the session's own user_id) for every balance read/write -- unchanged by this pass; the expert-safe-messaging fix is purely a ChatWindow display concern, not a change to this billing logic. Locks the session row, computes elapsed/overage minutes from wall-clock time vs started_at, transitions active -> grace -> ended per session_rules.md. endSession() permanently ends a session (now explicitly the 'End Session' button's contract, distinct from the new soft 'Leave' which makes no server call at all). New listPastSessionsForClient(userId) / listPastSessionsForExpert(expertId): ended sessions only, mirroring the listAssignedBookings-vs-listAllAssignedBookings own-vs-all asymmetry, feeding /app/chats. Also exports assertSessionAccess(callerId, sessionId) -- reused by messages.ts, the Pusher auth endpoint, and now the chat page (to compute isViewerExpert for ChatWindow).

components.name: email lib
components.description: sendBookingNotificationEmail() builds a plain-text .txt attachment (client name, DOB/time/place-of-birth with 'Not provided' fallbacks, and the booking note) and sends it via Resend to the assigned expert. notifyExpertOfBooking(clientUserId, booking) is the entry point called from POST /api/bookings after a successful createBooking -- fire-and-forget, console.error only on failure, never affects the booking response (same pattern as messages.ts's Pusher trigger()). The Resend client is constructed lazily inside a function, NOT at module load time -- Resend's constructor throws synchronously on a missing API key, and doing that eagerly at import time took down the entire POST /api/bookings route (a real bug caught and fixed during a prior feature pass, before RESEND_API_KEY was ever set). If the key is absent, sends are skipped with a clear console.error rather than crashing anything. New (2026-07-04): sendPasswordResetEmail({toEmail, resetUrl}) -- same lazy-client-null-check-and-skip pattern, a simple text email with the reset link, no orchestrator layer needed since the caller already has both pieces of data in hand.

components.name: passwordReset lib
components.description: New. createPasswordResetToken(userId): generates a crypto.randomBytes(32) token (256 bits of entropy), stores only its SHA-256 hash (never the plaintext) in the new password_reset_tokens table with a 1-hour expiry, first deleting any prior unused token for that user (also enforced at the DB level by a partial unique index on (user_id) WHERE used_at IS NULL) -- returns the plaintext token, which only ever exists in the HTTP response/email link. resetPasswordWithToken(token, newPassword): hashes the incoming token, looks up a matching unexpired/unused row with a FOR UPDATE row lock (guards a double-submit race on the same link), throws InvalidOrExpiredTokenError if none found, else bcrypt-hashes the new password (10 rounds, matching createUser) and atomically updates users.password_hash + marks the token used_at. SHA-256 (not bcrypt) is the correct choice for the token itself -- bcrypt's slow-hash design defends against brute-forcing a low-entropy human-chosen secret (a password), which doesn't apply to a 256-bit random token.

components.name: pusherServer
components.description: Singleton server-side Pusher client (app id/key/secret/cluster from env, gitignored .env.local), cached on globalThis in dev like db.ts's pool. Used by messages.ts to trigger 'new-message' events and by the Pusher auth route to sign channel subscriptions.

components.name: pusherClient
components.description: Client-side Pusher singleton (getPusherClient()), configured with the public key/cluster (NEXT_PUBLIC_ env vars) and authEndpoint '/api/pusher/auth' for private-channel subscriptions. Imported by ChatWindow.tsx.

components.name: messages lib
components.description: listMessages(callerId, sessionId): authorizes via assertSessionAccess, returns all messages for a session oldest-first with sender name/role joined in. sendMessage(callerId, sessionId, body): authorizes, inserts into the messages table, then pusherServer.trigger()s a 'new-message' event on that session's private channel (private-session-{sessionId}) with the persisted row -- broadcast to every subscriber including the sender, so the client dedupes by message id rather than appending optimistically. Throws EmptyMessageError for blank input.

apiRoutes.description: NextAuth with CredentialsProvider (email + password, verifyUser()) and GoogleProvider (real client ID/secret from env, PKCE). JWT session strategy; jwt callback links Google sign-ins to the real users table via findOrCreateOAuthUser() (no DB adapter is used, so this linking is done by hand) and always sets token.id to our own UUID, never Google's subject id. session callback propagates that onto session.user.id (typed via src/types/next-auth.d.ts).
Validates email/password (min 8 chars), calls createUser(). Returns 201 + public user on success, 409 if email already registered, 400 for missing/short fields.

New. No auth. Body { email }. Looks up the user; if found, calls createPasswordResetToken() and fires sendPasswordResetEmail() (fire-and-forget) with a ${NEXTAUTH_URL}/user/reset-password?token=... link. ALWAYS returns the same generic 200 { message: "If an account exists for that email, we've sent a reset link." } regardless of whether the email matched an account or the send succeeded -- this is the actual non-enumeration security property, verified directly by diffing the response (status/body) for a real vs. a definitely-nonexistent email. No rate limiting -- flagged as a known, deliberately-deferred gap, not an oversight (this codebase has no rate-limiting infrastructure anywhere yet).

New. No auth. Body { token, newPassword } (>=8 chars, matching /api/register's rule). Calls resetPasswordWithToken(). 400 with a generic "This reset link is invalid or has expired." for InvalidOrExpiredTokenError (covers not-found, already-used, and expired -- deliberately not distinguished in the response, same non-enumeration principle as forgot-password).

New. Authenticated (getServerSession, same 401 guard as /api/user/profile). Body { currentPassword?, newPassword }. Calls changePassword(). 403 with "Current password is incorrect." for InvalidCurrentPasswordError (only thrown when the account already has a password_hash and currentPassword is missing or doesn't match); a Google-only account (no existing password_hash) can set one with no currentPassword at all.

Returns { balance } for the signed-in user via getBalance(). 401 if not authenticated. Intended for client-side polling later; the dashboard/chat pages currently call getBalance()/tickSession() directly server-side instead of hitting this.

Body { availabilitySlotId, note? }. Calls createBooking(), then best-effort (fire-and-forget, never blocks the response) calls notifyExpertOfBooking() to email the assigned expert a .txt with the client's birth details + note. 201 + booking on success, 402 if insufficient credits, 404 if the slot doesn't exist, 409 if it's no longer open/in the future, 401 if unauthenticated.

New. GET returns { profile } via getUserProfile(). PATCH body { name, dateOfBirth, timeOfBirth, placeOfBirth } (always the full current state, not partial fields, since the profile form is a controlled form re-submitting everything) calls updateUserProfile(), returns { profile }. Both 401 if unauthenticated; no other validation beyond loose type-checking (this codebase has no schema-validation library anywhere, a malformed date just bubbles up as a Postgres error -> 500).

Body { newAvailabilitySlotId }. Calls rescheduleBooking(). Both the client and the assigned expert can call it (only the client is subject to the 3-day lead-time rule). 200 + updated booking; 404 for not-found (booking or new slot), 403 for neither-client-nor-expert, 409 for not-scheduled / lead-time-expired / new-slot-not-available-or-wrong-type.

Body { bookingId }. Calls joinSession(); for the booking's client, creates a chat_sessions row (or resumes an existing non-ended one) and marks the booking 'active'; for the assigned expert, only attaches to an existing session. 200 + { sessionId }, 404/403/409 for not-found / neither client-nor-expert / not-joinable / (expert-only) session-not-started-yet / (client-only, new) too-early-to-join.

Body { slotType, startsAt }. Expert-only (role !== 'expert' -> 403, first route in the app to gate on role). Calls createAvailabilitySlot(). 201 + slot; 409 if it overlaps an existing slot, 400 if startsAt isn't in the future or slotType is invalid.
Expert-only. Calls cancelAvailabilitySlot() -- only works on a still-'open' (unbooked) slot. 200 on success; 404/403/409 for not-found/not-yours/not-open.

The billing heartbeat -- polled by ChatWindow every 5s. Calls tickSession(); charges overage as needed and returns the current SessionState (balance, status, grace countdown, etc). 404/403 for not-found/not-yours.

Permanently ends a session (marks chat_sessions 'ended' and the booking 'completed'). Now explicitly the 'End Session' button's contract in ChatWindow (window.confirm-guarded) -- the separate 'Leave' button makes no call to this endpoint at all, it's pure client-side navigation, which is what makes an accidental disconnect recoverable via Join again.

Calls cancelBooking(). Returns { refundPercent, refundAmount } on success. 404/403/409 for not-found/not-yours/not-cancellable (already active or finished). Called by BookingActions' 'Cancel' button, shown only for 'scheduled' (not yet joined) bookings.

GET returns { messages } via listMessages() (404/403 for not-found/not-yours). POST { text } validates length (<=2000 chars, non-empty) and calls sendMessage(), which persists and broadcasts over Pusher; returns the saved message, 201. Both endpoints share the exact same client-or-assigned-expert authorization as billing (via assertSessionAccess).

Streams the full message history for a session as a downloadable .txt file (Content-Disposition: attachment), one line per message formatted as '[timestamp] Sender: body'. Plain <a href> link in ChatWindow -- no JS needed, the browser's normal same-origin cookie auth + the Content-Disposition header handle the download. Same access rule as everything else (client or assigned expert only).

Pusher's required channel-authorization endpoint for private channels. Reads socket_id/channel_name from the form-encoded request Pusher's client SDK sends, validates the channel is a 'private-session-{id}' one, runs the same assertSessionAccess check, then returns pusherServer.authorizeChannel()'s signed response. This is what actually prevents an unrelated user from eavesdropping on a session's live messages -- verified directly: an unrelated third account got 403 from this endpoint (and from GET/POST messages and the export route).

plannedApiRoutes.description: Create a Razorpay order for a credit pack. See razorpay_flow.md step 2.
Verify Razorpay webhook signature, idempotently credit the wallet ledger. See razorpay_flow.md step 4.

tables.name: users
tables.purpose: Account records (id UUID, email, name, role: 'user' | 'expert', plus date_of_birth/time_of_birth/place_of_birth profile fields added 2026-07-04, all nullable)

tables.name: ledger_entries
tables.purpose: Append-only credit ledger; balance = SUM(amount) per user

tables.name: payments
tables.purpose: Razorpay order/payment records with status tracking

tables.name: availability_slots
tables.purpose: The expert's calendar -- a row is a bookable window (expert_id, slot_type, starts_at, ends_at, status: 'open'/'booked'/'cancelled'). Added 2026-07-03.

tables.name: bookings
tables.purpose: A client's claim on an availability slot (starts_at, ends_at, included_minutes, expert_id, availability_slot_id, slot_type -- all snapshotted at booking time, never re-derived live; plus an optional client-written note added 2026-07-04)

tables.name: chat_sessions
tables.purpose: Active/grace/ended chat sessions tied to a booking; grace_started_at (added 2026-07-01) tracks the 2-minute top-up window after balance hits 0

tables.name: messages
tables.purpose: Persisted chat history (session_id, sender_id, body, created_at), added 2026-07-03. Source of truth for both the live Pusher broadcast and the .txt export -- Pusher itself does not retain history.

tables.name: password_reset_tokens
tables.purpose: Short-lived, single-use password reset tokens (user_id, token_hash -- SHA-256, plaintext never stored, expires_at, used_at). A partial unique index on (user_id) WHERE used_at IS NULL enforces at most one live token per user at the DB level. Added 2026-07-04.

database.notes: Live Neon Postgres database. schema.sql applied 2026-07-01 -- all 8 tables exist and are in active use (users, ledger_entries, bookings, availability_slots, chat_sessions, messages, password_reset_tokens); payments is still empty, waiting on Razorpay. Schema additions beyond the original draft: users.password_hash (nullable, Credentials + Google), chat_sessions.grace_started_at (grace-period tracking), users.role and bookings.expert_id (expert feature), messages table (real-time chat), availability_slots table + bookings.availability_slot_id/slot_type (2026-07-03, the calendar/scheduling feature), users.date_of_birth/time_of_birth/place_of_birth + bookings.note (2026-07-04, profile + booking-notification feature), password_reset_tokens table (2026-07-04, forgot/reset password feature). One demo expert account is seeded directly in the DB (email expert@astrochat.demo, role='expert') -- not creatable via the public signup form. createBooking() auto-assigns whichever user has role='expert' to every new booking (there's only ever been the one) via the slot's expert_id. Connection string lives in .env.local (gitignored) as DATABASE_URL; src/lib/db.ts exports a pooled client + withTransaction() helper. Note: an older ad-hoc 'users' table (integer id, username/password, 2 rows) existed in this DB before schema.sql was applied and was intentionally dropped in favor of the UUID/email schema. Also note: none of the FKs here (ledger_entries.user_id, chat_sessions.booking_id, messages.session_id, bookings.availability_slot_id, password_reset_tokens.user_id) have ON DELETE CASCADE, so deleting a row with dependents requires deleting those first (FK is RESTRICT by default). Also note: node-postgres parses a DATE column into a JS Date object at LOCAL midnight by default, not a string -- getUserProfile()/updateUserProfile() normalize this with local-date getters (getFullYear/getMonth/getDate), NOT toISOString() (which converts to UTC and silently shifts the calendar date back a day in any timezone ahead of UTC -- a real bug caught and fixed during the profile feature's verification).

userFlows.name: Sign up / Log in (email+password)
steps: User fills form on /user/signup (name, email, password, confirm) or /user/login (email, password)
Signup: POST /api/register creates the user (bcrypt-hashed password) in Postgres, then auto-calls signIn('credentials')
Login: signIn('credentials') -> NextAuth authorize() -> verifyUser() checks email + bcrypt.compare() against the DB
NextAuth session created (JWT strategy, session.user.id populated)
Header switches to authenticated state, shows Dashboard link; redirect to /app/dashboard on success

userFlows.name: Sign up / Log in with Google
steps: User clicks 'Continue with Google' on /user/login or /user/signup -> signIn('google', { callbackUrl: '/app/dashboard' })

Full-page redirect to Google's OAuth consent screen (accounts.google.com) with PKCE; verified the redirect URL carries the correct client_id and a redirect_uri of http://localhost:3000/api/auth/callback/google matching what's registered in Google Cloud Console
[Requires a real interactive Google login -- not completed by an automated test] User approves consent, Google redirects back to /api/auth/callback/google with an auth code

NextAuth exchanges the code for a profile (email, name); the jwt callback (account.provider === 'google') calls findOrCreateOAuthUser(email, name) -- links to an existing users row with that email (created via password signup) or creates a new password-less one
token.id / session.user.id is always our own DB UUID, never Google's subject id, so downstream code (balance, bookings, sessions) works identically regardless of how the user signed in

Verified the linking logic directly against the DB: a brand-new Google email creates one row (repeat sign-ins reuse it, no duplicates); signing in with Google using an email that already has a password-based account links to that same row without touching its password_hash, so the user can still log in either way afterward

userFlows.name: Forgot password / reset password
steps: User clicks 'Forgot password?' on /user/login -> /user/forgot-password, enters their email, POSTs to /api/forgot-password

Server looks up the email; if it matches a user, creates a single-use token (256-bit random, only its SHA-256 hash ever stored) with a 1-hour expiry and best-effort emails a reset link via Resend -- but ALWAYS returns the same generic success message regardless of whether the email matched or the send succeeded, so the endpoint can't be used to check which emails are registered

User clicks the emailed link -> /user/reset-password?token=... (a Suspense-wrapped client component, a Next.js 15 App Router requirement for reading the token via useSearchParams()), enters a new password twice

POSTs to /api/reset-password, which hashes the incoming token, looks up a matching unexpired/unused row with a row lock, and only then updates users.password_hash and marks the token used -- a mismatched, expired, or already-used token all map to the same generic 'invalid or expired' error, again to avoid leaking which failure mode occurred
Redirects to /user/login on success

Verified end-to-end: happy path (request -> reset -> log in with the new password all confirmed working); expired-token rejection (backdated a token's expires_at directly, confirmed 400); used-token replay rejection (resubmitted a spent token, confirmed 400); non-enumeration (diffed the response for a real vs. a definitely-nonexistent email -- identical status and body).

userFlows.name: Change password / set a first password
steps: User opens /app/profile and sees a 'Change Password' panel (if their account already has a password) or a 'Set a Password' panel (if it's a Google-only account with no password_hash yet)

Change Password requires the current password, verified server-side via bcrypt.compare against the real password_hash (never trusted from the client); Set a Password has no current-password field at all, since there's nothing to verify against
Both variants require a new password + confirmation that must match client-side (rejected with zero network call on mismatch) before POSTing to /api/user/password
On success, all three fields clear and a confirmation notice shows -- for the Set-a-Password path, the notice explicitly mentions that email/password login is now also available

Verified end-to-end: wrong current password correctly rejected (403); mismatched new/confirm correctly rejected client-side with no request sent; correct change-password flow succeeded and the new password was confirmed to actually work by logging out and back in with it; the Set-a-Password path was verified against a real Google-only test account (temporarily given credentials to establish a session, then reverted to password_hash=NULL server-side to exercise the true no-current-password branch) and confirmed the resulting password_hash was set correctly and matched the submitted password.

userFlows.name: View credit balance
steps: Dashboard and chat pages call getServerSession(authOptions) to identify the user (redirect to /user/login if none)
getBalance(userId) runs SELECT COALESCE(SUM(amount),0) FROM ledger_entries WHERE user_id = $1
Dashboard shows the real number instead of a hardcoded 350; chat page passes it as ChatWindow's initialCredits instead of a hardcoded 10
GET /api/user/balance exposes the same number for future client-side polling (e.g. live updates during a session)

userFlows.name: Buy credits
steps: User views packs on /pricing
[MISSING] Click 'Buy Now' -> POST /api/payments/create-order
[PLANNED] Razorpay Checkout modal opens with order_id
[PLANNED] Razorpay webhook -> /api/webhooks/razorpay verifies signature, credits ledger_entries
Once credited, the balance immediately shows correctly (verified by manually inserting ledger_entries rows and confirming the dashboard/chat page reflect the new sum)

userFlows.name: Book & join a chat session
steps: Client clicks 'Find a Slot' on the dashboard -> /app/slots, which lists every open calendar slot across all experts (SlotFinder island, from listOpenSlotsWithExpertNames()), grouped by date, with real date/time/type/price/expert name

Clicking a slot's Book button reveals an inline note textarea (optional -- questions/life-sectors for the expert) rather than booking instantly; if the client's profile is missing birth details, a non-blocking hint suggests filling in /app/profile or just adding them to the note

Confirming POSTs {availabilitySlotId, note} to /api/bookings, which claims the specific slot: locks it, verifies it's still open and in the future, debits the wallet, creates the booking (snapshotting starts_at/ends_at/included_minutes/slot_type/note from the slot), inside one transaction guarded by a per-user advisory lock and a row lock on the slot

After the booking succeeds, the server best-effort emails the assigned expert (via Resend) a .txt attachment with the client's name/DOB/time/place-of-birth ('Not provided' for any gap) and the note -- fire-and-forget, never blocks or fails the booking response even if RESEND_API_KEY isn't set (verified: a missing key logs a clear message and the booking still succeeds, rather than the whole route crashing, which was a real bug caught and fixed during this pass)
Booking does NOT auto-join -- the slot may be scheduled well in the future. It appears on the dashboard's Sessions list with Join disabled ('Not yet') until within 10 minutes of its real starts_at

Once within the join window: POST /api/sessions/join creates a chat_sessions row (started_at = NOW(), the actual click time) and marks the booking 'active', then the browser navigates to /app/chat/[sessionId]

Chat page calls tickSession() server-side for the authoritative initial state; ChatWindow then polls POST /api/sessions/[id]/tick every 5s, charging overage per session_rules.md and transitioning active -> grace -> ended

'Leave' (soft, just navigates away, no server call -- either party can Join again later to resume the exact same session) and 'End Session' (hard, window.confirm-guarded, permanently ends it for both parties) are now two separate buttons in ChatWindow

Verified end-to-end: booked a slot ~20 min out with a note, confirmed the note persisted and the email pipeline attempted correctly; confirmed Join stayed disabled then became clickable once backdated into the 10-min window; confirmed soft-Leave makes no /end call and the same session (with prior messages intact) resumes on rejoin; confirmed End Session permanently completes the booking and removes it from the upcoming list.

userFlows.name: Cancel a booking
steps: Client cancels a not-yet-joined ('scheduled') booking from BookingActions, or the expert cancels ANY of their assigned scheduled bookings from AdminBookingsList on /app/admin -- both call the same POST /api/bookings/[id]/cancel -> cancelBooking()

Refund policy now depends on who's cancelling: client-initiated keeps the original tiered policy (100% if >24h before starts_at, 50% otherwise, 0% no-show past ends_at unjoined); expert-initiated is ALWAYS 100%, regardless of timing -- business-initiated, not the client's fault

Refund (if any) always credits the client's ledger (booking.user_id), never whichever party actually clicked cancel; booking status -> 'cancelled'; the tied availability_slots row releases back to 'open' if its time hasn't passed, freeing it for another client to book
Attempting to cancel an already-'active' booking returns 409 ('leave it instead') and an already-finished booking also 409, for either caller

Verified end-to-end: client-cancelled a >24h-out booking (100%) confirming the original tiered logic still works unchanged; separately booked a <24h-out slot and cancelled it AS THE EXPERT, confirming a full 100% refund (which would have been 50% if the client had cancelled it) landed correctly on the client's ledger with zero ledger activity on the expert's own account; confirmed the freed slot reappeared as 'open' after each cancel.

userFlows.name: Reschedule a booking
steps: New capability. Either the client (from their dashboard) or the assigned expert (from /app/admin) can reschedule a 'scheduled' booking to a different open slot of the SAME slot_type -- POST /api/bookings/[id]/reschedule -> rescheduleBooking()

Client-initiated reschedules require the CURRENT booking to start more than 3 days from now (RescheduleWindowExpiredError / 409 otherwise) -- the assigned expert is exempt from this restriction and can reschedule however soon the session is

The new slot must be open, in the future, the same type, and the same expert; the old slot releases back to 'open', the new one is claimed, and the booking's starts_at/ends_at update to match -- no ledger entries at all, since cost/duration never change via reschedule

Verified end-to-end: client successfully rescheduled a booking scheduled >3 days out; a client attempt to reschedule a <3-days-out booking was correctly rejected with 409; the SAME booking was then successfully rescheduled by the expert instead, proving the exemption; a cross-type reschedule attempt (standard -> extended) was correctly rejected.

userFlows.name: Expert manages their availability calendar
steps: New capability, on /app/admin (expert-only -- non-experts are redirected to /app/dashboard, and the two new admin API routes return 403 for non-experts, verified directly)

Expert creates a slot (slot type dropdown + datetime-local picker, converted to a proper ISO timestamp client-side to avoid timezone ambiguity) -> POST /api/admin/availability-slots -> createAvailabilitySlot(): validates the time is in the future and doesn't overlap an existing slot, inserts status='open'

Expert can cancel a still-open (unbooked) slot directly -> POST /api/admin/availability-slots/[id]/cancel; a booked slot can only be freed by cancelling/rescheduling the booking that claimed it, not the slot itself
Newly created open slots appear immediately on clients' dashboards as bookable options (both pages read from the same availability_slots table)

Verified end-to-end: created three slots (two standard, one extended) as the expert, confirmed they appeared correctly (name, time, price) on a client's dashboard in chronological order, confirmed booking one flipped it to 'booked' and removed it from the bookable list.

userFlows.name: View ledger history
steps: User clicks 'Ledger History' in the dashboard's Quick Actions (now a real Link, not a dead button) -> /app/ledger
listLedgerEntries(userId) returns every ledger_entries row for the user, newest first
Each row shows a human label (Credit purchase / Slot booking / Overage charge / Refund), the free-text description, timestamp, and the signed amount (green +N, red -N)
Verified end-to-end: a test user's purchase (+200) and slot booking (-50) both appeared correctly labeled, ordered, and colored.

userFlows.name: Expert views and joins an assigned session
steps: A booking is auto-assigned to the (one, seeded) demo expert at creation time (bookings.expert_id)

Expert logs in with the seeded demo account (role='expert') and sees a completely different dashboard: 'Client Sessions' listing bookings assigned to them (client name, slot length, time), no wallet/quick-actions
While the booking is still 'scheduled' (client hasn't joined yet), the expert sees 'Waiting for client' instead of a Join button

Once the client joins (booking -> 'active'), the expert's 'Join Session' button becomes active; clicking it calls the same POST /api/sessions/join, which for an expert caller only ever attaches to the client's already-existing chat_sessions row (never creates or bills one)

The expert lands on the exact same /app/chat/[id] session as the client and sees the same live billing state (balance, status, grace countdown) -- verified this reflects the client's real wallet (e.g. 100 credits), not the expert's own (which would be 0, since experts never accrue ledger_entries)

Verified end-to-end in browser: booked and joined as the client, switched accounts, confirmed the expert dashboard listed the session correctly (both before and after the client joined), joined as the expert, and confirmed the shared session state matched the client's.

userFlows.name: Real-time chat messaging + export
steps: Chat page loads message history server-side (listMessages) and passes it to ChatWindow as initialMessages, alongside the viewer's own userId

ChatWindow subscribes to the session's private Pusher channel (private-session-{sessionId}) via a client-side Pusher connection authorized through POST /api/pusher/auth (which enforces the same client-or-assigned-expert rule as billing)

Sending a message POSTs to /api/sessions/[id]/messages, which persists it to the messages table and triggers a 'new-message' Pusher event to that channel; the sender does NOT append it optimistically -- it only appears once the real Pusher event round-trips back, which is itself the proof the live pipeline works end-to-end rather than a local echo

Own vs. other-party messages are visually distinguished (right/accent vs. left/white bubble) by comparing each message's senderId to the viewer's own id, not a fixed role -- so the same UI works correctly for both the client and the expert viewing the same session
'Export Chat' is a plain link to GET /api/sessions/[id]/export, which streams the full history as a downloadable chat-{id}.txt file (timestamp, sender name, message per line)

Verified end-to-end in browser: sent a message and watched it arrive purely via the Pusher round-trip (no optimistic UI); reloaded the page and confirmed it persisted; sent a second message and fetched the export endpoint directly, confirming both messages appeared correctly formatted with the right Content-Type/Content-Disposition headers; created an unrelated third account and confirmed it got 403 from all four surfaces (list messages, send, export, and the Pusher channel-auth endpoint itself -- so an outsider can't even subscribe to eavesdrop).
Also verified this pass: the input box keeps focus after every send (checked document.activeElement directly) -- previously required re-clicking the box before typing the next message.

userFlows.name: Manage profile
steps: User opens /app/profile from the dashboard's Quick Actions
ProfileForm loads the current name/DOB/time-of-birth/place-of-birth (or blank if never set) and PATCHes /api/user/profile on save -- never required, never blocks any other flow

Verified end-to-end: saved DOB/time/place, reloaded the page, confirmed the exact values persisted through the DB round-trip (this caught and fixed a real bug where the saved date displayed one day earlier than entered, due to a timezone conversion issue in how Postgres DATE columns are parsed by node-postgres).

userFlows.name: View past chats
steps: User clicks 'Past Chats' on the dashboard -> /app/chats
Clients see their own ended sessions; experts see every ended session across every client they've ever had, mirroring the same asymmetry as their booking lists
Each row links to /app/chat/[id] (already renders read-only for an ended session -- no new code needed there) and to the existing .txt export endpoint
Verified end-to-end: as the expert, confirmed two ended sessions with the same client showed up correctly with date/duration/type; View opened the correct read-only transcript.

userFlows.name: Rejoin a session after an accidental leave or connection drop
steps: Previously, the single 'Leave' button called the same permanent end-session endpoint as intentionally finishing a consultation -- an accidental click (or, effectively, any disconnect that happened to trigger it) irreversibly completed the booking for both parties

Now 'Leave' is purely client-side navigation (no server call at all) -- the chat_sessions row stays exactly as it was ('active' or 'grace'), so either party can simply click Join/Resume again from their dashboard to reattach to the identical session, with all prior messages intact
'End Session' is the new, explicit, confirmed (window.confirm) action for permanently finishing a consultation -- unchanged in effect from the old 'Leave', just no longer reachable by accident

Billing is unaffected either way: it's driven by wall-clock time (now - started_at), not by whether the tab was open, so a session left soft for a while still bills/graces/ends correctly based on real elapsed time the next time it's ticked

Verified end-to-end: clicked Leave, confirmed via network inspection that no /end request fired, then rejoined and confirmed the same session id and both prior messages were still there; separately clicked End Session, confirmed the confirmation prompt, and confirmed the booking became permanently 'completed' and un-rejoinable.

userFlows.name: Expert sees a neutral message when a client's session ends (not a credit-shortfall prompt)
steps: BUG (now fixed): when a session ended -- whether from the client running out of credits or either party ending it -- the expert's identical ChatWindow instance showed the exact same 'Session ended, you ran out of credits, Top Up Credits' message, which is nonsensical since experts never have a credit balance

Root cause: SessionState (from tickSession) always reflects the client's wallet by design (correct for billing), but the UI rendered that balance-aware copy unconditionally regardless of who was viewing

Fix: the chat page now computes isViewerExpert (via the existing assertSessionAccess() check) and passes it to ChatWindow, which uses it purely for display -- an expert viewer sees a neutral 'This session has ended.' with no credit language and no Top Up button; the client viewer is unaffected

Verified end-to-end: ended a session, opened it as the expert and confirmed the neutral message with no Top Up button, then confirmed the client viewing the same session still saw the original credit-aware copy.

knownGaps: payments table still unused -- no Razorpay integration code yet (design only, in razorpay_flow.md), so there is still no real way to add credits other than a manual DB insert
Pricing page 'Buy Now' buttons have no onClick handlers
Chat messages are plain text only -- no file/image attachments, no read receipts, no typing indicators, no message editing/deletion

Pusher is on its free tier with no monitoring/alerting set up -- if usage grows past free-tier limits (200k msgs/day, 100 concurrent connections) messages would start failing silently from the client's point of view (trigger() would throw server-side, logged via console.error, but nothing surfaces that to the user yet)

Only one expert exists (seeded demo account) and every booking auto-assigns to them -- no real onboarding flow for additional experts, no admin UI to create/manage them, and the public signup form has no way to become an expert (by design, but worth noting as a gap if multi-expert support is ever wanted); AdminBookingsList/AvailabilityManager both implicitly assume the logged-in expert IS the sole admin. The new Slot Finder page (/app/slots) and its listOpenSlotsWithExpertNames() query ARE already multi-expert-ready, though -- this gap is purely 'no second expert has ever been created,' not a code limitation anymore.
Availability slots are created one at a time, manually, by the expert -- no recurring weekly-pattern generation (e.g. 'available Mon-Fri 9-5'), deliberately deferred
No way to edit an existing open slot's time in place -- only cancel + create a new one covers that; cross-type reschedule is also blocked by design (would require a price/duration delta mid-booking)

Slot pricing (SLOT_TYPES: standard/extended) is still a hardcoded constant, not admin-editable -- dynamic per-tier pricing was explicitly scoped out of the calendar feature (a real, larger, deferred piece of work)

datetime-local slot creation relies on the browser's local timezone via new Date(value).toISOString() -- correct for a single-timezone demo, but worth revisiting if the expert and clients are ever expected to be in meaningfully different timezones (no explicit timezone display/conversion in the UI)

No email verification or rate limiting on /api/register or the credentials login. Password reset now exists (2026-07-04) but ALSO has no rate limiting -- /api/forgot-password is an unauthenticated, always-200 endpoint with no throttling, making it a free-form email-sending trigger unless abused deliberately; deferred as a conscious trade-off (this codebase has zero rate-limiting infrastructure anywhere) rather than built as new infra for this one feature. The 1-hour token expiry and delete-prior-unused-token behavior are the only mitigations in place.
No migration tool -- schema changes (password_hash, grace_started_at) are applied via one-off scripts, not tracked migrations
JWT session strategy means a deleted/disabled user's session stays valid until the token expires (30-day default) -- session isn't re-checked against the DB on every request
ledger_entries.user_id and chat_sessions.booking_id have no ON DELETE CASCADE -- deleting a user/booking with dependent rows requires deleting those rows first (FK is RESTRICT by default)

isLowBalance (<=4 credits, from session_rules.md's 'low balance warning') is implemented and wired into the UI but wasn't exercised in the live test -- only the grace-period and hard-stop paths were explicitly verified

Google OAuth was verified only up to the redirect to Google's consent screen (correct client_id/redirect_uri) plus the account-linking DB logic in isolation -- the full interactive round trip (consent screen -> callback -> real session) needs a real Google account and hasn't been exercised end-to-end. Also: while the Google Cloud OAuth consent screen is in 'Testing' publishing status, only emails added as test users in that screen can actually complete the flow -- anyone else will be blocked by Google before reaching the app.

RESEND_API_KEY is not yet set -- booking-notification emails are code-complete and verified to fail gracefully (booking still succeeds, a clear message is logged) but have never actually been sent. The user needs to create a Resend account, get an API key, and add it to .env.local before real delivery, attachment formatting, and sandbox-sender recipient restrictions can be verified.

Booking notes and profile birth-details are never shown anywhere in-app (dashboard, admin bookings list, past chats) -- they only ever leave the system via the one-shot booking-notification email. An expert who didn't receive/read that email (or whose RESEND_API_KEY wasn't set at booking time) has no in-app way to see a client's note or birth details after the fact.

Profile fields (name/DOB/time/place of birth) are entirely optional and never enforced -- a client can book indefinitely without ever filling them in, by design, but this means the booking-notification email can be persistently useless for an expert if clients never bother.

changelog.change: Created project-flow.json and project-flow.html trackers; documented current app state (UI scaffold on Next.js, auth/payments/DB/chat all mocked or design-only).

Connected to a live Neon Postgres DB (src/lib/db.ts, DATABASE_URL in .env.local). Found and dropped a pre-existing incompatible 'users' table (int id, username/password), then applied schema.sql fresh -- all 5 tables now live and empty.

Wired real authentication: added password_hash column to users, src/lib/users.ts (createUser/verifyUser with bcrypt), POST /api/register, rewrote NextAuth route to verify real credentials (removed the unconfigured GoogleProvider and the hardcoded John Doe user), wired signup/login pages to call these. Verified end-to-end in browser: signup creates a real DB row and auto-logs in, wrong password is rejected, duplicate email returns 409, correct password logs in.

Wired real credit balance: moved NextAuthOptions into src/lib/auth.ts (shared, reusable), added src/lib/ledger.ts (getBalance), GET /api/user/balance, and auth guards + real balance on /app/dashboard and /app/chat/[id] (replacing hardcoded 350 and 10). Verified end-to-end: unauthenticated users redirect to /user/login; a fresh signup shows 0 credits; manually inserting ledger_entries rows (+250 purchase, -50 booking) immediately shows 200 on both the dashboard and the chat page after refresh.

Built the full booking + session-join + server-authoritative billing loop per session_rules.md: src/lib/sessionRules.ts (constants), src/lib/bookings.ts (createBooking with a per-user advisory lock, listUpcomingBookings), src/lib/sessions.ts (joinSession, tickSession -- the billing engine, endSession), POST /api/bookings, POST /api/sessions/join, POST /api/sessions/[id]/tick, POST /api/sessions/[id]/end. Added chat_sessions.grace_started_at column. New BookingActions island wired into the dashboard (replacing the hardcoded session); ChatWindow rewritten to poll the tick endpoint every 5s instead of a fake client-side timer, plus a 'Leave' button. Verified end-to-end in browser, including forcing overage/grace/hard-stop by backdating started_at and grace_started_at in the DB: overage billed correctly (4 min over -> 8 credits), grace period triggered exactly at balance <= 0 with an accurate countdown, hard stop fired after the grace window ended (booking flipped to 'completed'), and a booking attempt with a negative balance was correctly rejected with 402. Also confirmed the background poll and manual test calls overlapping never double-charged, validating the row-lock + ledger-derived 'already billed' design.

Added booking cancellation with tiered refunds per session_rules.md: cancelBooking() in src/lib/bookings.ts, POST /api/bookings/[id]/cancel, and a Cancel button in BookingActions (shown only for not-yet-joined bookings). Refactored BookingNotFoundError/ForbiddenError to live in bookings.ts as the single source, re-exported from sessions.ts (previously duplicated). Verified all three refund tiers end-to-end (50% default, 100% when cancelled >24h ahead, 0% no-show) plus the 409 block on cancelling an already-active session -- balance and the dashboard list updated correctly after each.

Added real Google sign-in/sign-up (user provided a Google Cloud OAuth Client ID/Secret, added to .env.local, gitignored). Re-added GoogleProvider to src/lib/auth.ts with real credentials; made users.password_hash nullable (schema.sql + live DB migration) since Google accounts don't set one; added findOrCreateOAuthUser() to src/lib/users.ts (atomic ON CONFLICT upsert) so a Google sign-in links to an existing password-based account by email instead of creating a duplicate, without touching that account's password; verifyUser() now guards against a NULL password_hash. Added a shared GoogleIcon component and 'Continue with Google' buttons to both /user/login and /user/signup. Verified: the button correctly redirects to accounts.google.com with the right client_id/redirect_uri/PKCE; the account-linking upsert logic verified directly against the DB (new user created, repeat sign-in reuses the same row, linking to an existing password account preserves that password). The full interactive Google consent round-trip needs a real Google login and wasn't completed by an automated test -- that's on the user to click through themselves.

Removed the dead dashboard 'Settings' quick action. Built out Ledger History as a real page (/app/ledger, src/lib/ledger.ts's new listLedgerEntries()) and wired the dashboard's 'Ledger History' quick action to link there instead of doing nothing. Added a basic expert role: users.role and bookings.expert_id columns (migrated live), one seeded demo expert account (real login, not creatable via public signup), createBooking() auto-assigns it to every new booking, and the dashboard now branches entirely by role -- clients see the existing wallet/booking view (now showing the assigned expert's name per booking), experts see a new 'Client Sessions' view (ExpertSessions island, src/lib/bookings.ts's new listAssignedBookings()) listing bookings assigned to them with a Join button that only activates once the client has started the session. Refactored src/lib/sessions.ts's joinSession/tickSession/endSession to distinguish the caller (client or assigned expert) from the payer (always the booking's client) so an expert can view/join a session without ever touching its billing -- verified live end-to-end: booked and joined as the client, switched to the seeded expert account, saw the assigned session (correctly gated on 'waiting for client' before the client joined), joined it as the expert once active, and confirmed the expert saw the client's real balance (not their own, which would be 0).

Replaced ChatWindow's local-only mock messages with real, persisted, real-time chat over Pusher Channels (user provided app_id/key/secret/cluster, stored in .env.local). Added the messages table; src/lib/pusherServer.ts and pusherClient.ts (singleton clients, mirroring the db.ts pattern); src/lib/messages.ts (listMessages/sendMessage, both authorized via a new assertSessionAccess() helper extracted from sessions.ts's existing client-or-assigned-expert rule, so chat access can't drift from billing access); GET/POST /api/sessions/[id]/messages; POST /api/pusher/auth (the private-channel authorization endpoint Pusher requires, gated by the same access check); GET /api/sessions/[id]/export (streams the conversation as a downloadable .txt). ChatWindow now loads history server-side, subscribes to the session's private channel, and renders messages as they arrive via the real Pusher event (deliberately not appended optimistically, so seeing a sent message appear is itself proof the round-trip works) with own/other styling based on senderId rather than a fixed role. Verified end-to-end: a message sent in-browser arrived purely through the Pusher round-trip; it persisted across a reload; the export endpoint returned a correctly formatted, correctly-headered .txt with both messages; and an unrelated third account was correctly rejected (403) from reading, sending, exporting, and even subscribing to the channel via the auth endpoint.

Replaced the 'book now, join now' model with a real calendar: new availability_slots table (expert-created, one at a time, open/booked/cancelled) and bookings.availability_slot_id/slot_type columns. src/lib/availability.ts (createAvailabilitySlot with overlap detection, listOpenSlots, listAllSlotsForExpert, cancelAvailabilitySlot). createBooking() reworked to claim a specific real slot (locks it, validates open+future, snapshots its time/type onto the booking) instead of instantiating starts_at=NOW(); POST /api/bookings body changed to {availabilitySlotId}. cancelBooking() refund policy now branches by caller: client keeps the original tiered policy, expert-initiated is ALWAYS 100% regardless of timing (a real behavioral fork, not just wider authorization) -- and the refund ledger entry now always credits booking.user_id rather than the caller, a correctness fix that mattered once the expert could also cancel. New rescheduleBooking() moves a 'scheduled' booking to a different open same-type slot; client-initiated reschedules require >3 days' lead time (RESCHEDULE_MIN_LEAD_TIME_MS), the expert is exempt. joinSession() gained a join-time gate (JOIN_WINDOW_BEFORE_MS, 10min) so a client can't start billing on a session long before its scheduled time; TooEarlyToJoinError. New /app/admin page (expert-only, first route gated on role) with AvailabilityManager (create/cancel slots) and AdminBookingsList (all bookings, cancel, reschedule) islands; new API routes POST /api/admin/availability-slots(+[id]/cancel) and POST /api/bookings/[id]/reschedule. BookingActions.tsx reworked to list real open slots instead of two hardcoded type buttons, gate Join client-side by time, and add an inline reschedule picker. Dynamic slot-type pricing was explicitly dropped from this pass per user feedback on the first draft of the plan. Caught and fixed a real bug during verification: booking used to auto-join immediately, which is wrong once bookings can be far in the future (the join attempt would just fail against the new time gate and silently strand the booking) -- fixed to book-then-refresh instead. Verified extensively end-to-end: join stays disabled until the 10-min window and then works with billing at real click time; client cancellation tiers unchanged; expert cancellation confirmed always-100% (including on a <24h booking that would've been 50% for the client) with the refund correctly landing on the client's ledger and zero activity on the expert's; reschedule succeeded for a >3-day-out booking and correctly failed for the client on a <3-day one while succeeding for the expert on the identical booking; cross-type reschedule and double-booking a slot both correctly rejected with 409; non-expert access to both new admin routes correctly rejected with 403.

Seven-item batch: replaced the dead 'Find Expert' dashboard button with a real Slot Finder page (/app/slots, SlotFinder island, new listOpenSlotsWithExpertNames() -- multi-expert-ready even though only one expert exists); added a Profile page (/app/profile, ProfileForm island, new users.date_of_birth/time_of_birth/place_of_birth columns + getUserProfile/updateUserProfile); added an optional booking note (bookings.note column) captured inline on the Slot Finder's booking flow; added Resend email integration (src/lib/email.ts, notifyExpertOfBooking()) so the assigned expert gets a .txt attachment with the client's birth details + note after every booking, fire-and-forget from POST /api/bookings; added a per-booking Past Chats page (/app/chats, new listPastSessionsForClient/listPastSessionsForExpert) with the same own-vs-all asymmetry as the booking lists; fixed the chat input losing focus after every send (ref + re-focus effect in ChatWindow.tsx); split ChatWindow's single permanent 'Leave' button into a soft 'Leave' (pure navigation, no API call, session stays resumable) and an explicit confirmed 'End Session' (the old permanent-end behavior) -- this is also the entire fix for accidental-disconnect rejoin, since joinSession's existing resume logic needed zero changes; fixed the expert seeing client-only 'you ran out of credits, top up' language when a session ended, by threading a new isViewerExpert flag (computed via assertSessionAccess in the chat page) into ChatWindow's ended/grace-state messaging. Caught and fixed two real bugs during verification, both before RESEND_API_KEY/real data existed to mask them: (1) the Resend client was constructed eagerly at module load, so its constructor throwing on a missing API key crashed the entire POST /api/bookings route, not just the email step -- fixed by constructing it lazily inside a function, gated on the key being present; (2) node-postgres parses a DATE column into a JS Date at LOCAL midnight, and the initial profile-normalization code used toISOString() (UTC), which silently shifted a saved date of birth back by one day in this server's timezone -- fixed by using local-date getters instead. Verified extensively end-to-end: Slot Finder booking with a note persisted correctly and the email pipeline attempted/skipped gracefully with a clear log message; profile fields round-tripped correctly through the DB after the date-parsing fix; chat input regained focus after every send (checked document.activeElement); soft-Leave made no /end call and rejoining resumed the identical session with prior messages intact; End Session permanently completed the booking with a confirmation prompt; the expert saw a neutral 'This session has ended.' with no credit language on an ended session while the client viewing the same session still saw the original credit-aware copy; Past Chats correctly showed the client's own sessions and the expert's sessions across multiple clients.

Added forgot/reset password (for logged-out users) and change/set password (for logged-in users, on /app/profile). New password_reset_tokens table (user_id, token_hash -- SHA-256 of a 256-bit random token, plaintext never stored -- expires_at, used_at, plus a partial unique index enforcing at most one live token per user at the DB level). New src/lib/passwordReset.ts (createPasswordResetToken, resetPasswordWithToken, both withTransaction-wrapped, the latter using a FOR UPDATE row lock to guard a double-submit race). users.ts gained changePassword() (verifies currentPassword via bcrypt if the account has one; skips that check entirely for a Google-only account setting its first password) and UserProfile.hasPassword. email.ts gained sendPasswordResetEmail() (same lazy-Resend-client pattern as the existing booking email). Three new routes: POST /api/forgot-password (always returns the same generic success message regardless of whether the email matched an account, to prevent user enumeration -- the actual security property, not incidental UX), POST /api/reset-password, POST /api/user/password. New pages /user/forgot-password and /user/reset-password (the latter needed a Suspense boundary around its useSearchParams() call, a real Next.js 15 App Router build requirement); a 'Forgot password?' link added to /user/login; a new ChangePasswordForm island rendered as a second panel on /app/profile. Verified extensively end-to-end: full happy path (request link -> reset -> log in with the new password); expired-token rejection (backdated a token's expires_at by its exact hash, not a time/status heuristic, since this is a shared live DB); used-token replay correctly rejected; non-enumeration confirmed by diffing the response for a real vs. a nonexistent email (identical status/body); change-password's wrong-current-password (403) and mismatched-confirmation (client-side, zero network calls) paths; and the Google-only set-a-password path, verified against a real test account by temporarily giving it a password to establish a session, then reverting to password_hash=NULL server-side to exercise the true no-current-password branch, confirming the resulting hash was set correctly. Known, deliberately-deferred gap: /api/forgot-password has no rate limiting (this codebase has none anywhere yet) -- the 1-hour token expiry and single-live-token-per-user invariant are the only mitigations.
